high_concern_flags.note: Alaska 2026 UI taxable wage base cited two ways across sources: $54,200 (Alaska Department of Labor official 2026-experience-rates page — PRIMARY) versus $49,700 (third-party payroll guides including alaskapayrollguide.com). Page uses $54,200 per the primary source. Third-party sources appear to be quoting a stale 2024/2025-style figure. Recommend monitoring at next Alaska rate-notice cycle (winter 2026) to confirm 2027 base.

Alaska sibling state-topic pages referenced in chip-nav and links-rail don't exist yet (state parent, wages-overtime, workers-comp, new-hire-reporting, employer-registrations, paid-leave-pto, termination-at-will, hiring-guide). Chip-nav entries rendered as anchors mirroring the Alabama/UK/Germany pattern; links-rail US-parent and Alaska-overview rendered as plain text per the link gate. Retrofit when wave-4 US-state mass build-out runs.

unique_angle: Alaska is the only US state where employees pay UI tax (0.5%). Lead with the no-state-income-tax + employee-pays-UI duality.
